toolPurpose: Open the Godot editor for a specified project. Initializes the editor environment and establishes the MCP bridge for subsequent tool operations.

project_path.description: Absolute filesystem path to the Godot project directory containing project.godot

toolPurpose: Execute a Godot project in debug mode with output capture. Optionally specify a scene to run. Injects the MCP runtime bridge for live interaction.

project_path.description: Absolute filesystem path to the Godot project directory

scene.description: Specific scene path to run (e.g., 'res://scenes/main.tscn'). If omitted, runs the project's main scene.

toolPurpose: Terminate a running Godot project instance. Cleanly shuts down the runtime and removes the MCP bridge.

project_path.description: Path to the project to stop (identifies the running instance)

toolPurpose: Retrieve console output, error messages, and debug logs from a running or recently-run project. Essential for diagnosing script errors and runtime issues.

project_path.description: Path to the project to get debug output from

toolPurpose: Retrieve detailed metadata about a Godot project including name, engine version, structure statistics (scene count, script count, resource count), and configuration.

project_path.description: Absolute filesystem path to the Godot project directory

toolPurpose: Discover Godot projects within a specified directory. Scans for project.godot files recursively and returns project metadata.

directory.description: Directory path to scan for Godot projects

recursive.description: Whether to search subdirectories recursively (default: true)

toolPurpose: Generate a new scene file with a specified root node type. Supports Node2D, Node3D, Control, and other Godot base types as the scene root.

project_path.description: Absolute path to the Godot project

scene_path.description: Path within the project for the new scene (e.g., 'res://scenes/level_01.tscn')

root_node_type.description: Node type for the scene root (e.g., 'Node2D', 'Node3D', 'Control', 'CharacterBody2D'). Default: 'Node2D'

toolPurpose: Insert a new node into an existing scene with configurable properties. Supports all Godot node types with initial property values for position, scale, color, etc.

project_path.description: Absolute path to the Godot project

scene_path.description: Path to the target scene file (e.g., 'res://scenes/main.tscn')

node_name.description: Name for the new node in the scene tree

parent_path.description: Path to the parent node (e.g., '.' for root, 'Player/Sprite'). Default: root node

properties.description: Initial property values as key-value pairs (e.g., {"position": {"x": 100, "y": 200}, "scale": {"x": 2, "y": 2}})

toolPurpose: Modify properties of an existing node in a scene. Update position, scale, rotation, colors, physics parameters, or any Godot node property.

project_path.description: Absolute path to the Godot project

scene_path.description: Path to the scene file containing the node

node_path.description: Path to the node within the scene tree (e.g., 'Player', 'World/Enemies/Boss')

properties.description: Property values to update as key-value pairs (e.g., {"position": {"x": 50, "y": 100}, "modulate": {"r": 1, "g": 0, "b": 0, "a": 1}})

toolPurpose: Remove a node and all its children from a scene. Use with caution as this operation is destructive.

project_path.description: Absolute path to the Godot project

scene_path.description: Path to the scene file containing the node

node_path.description: Path to the node to delete within the scene tree

toolPurpose: Read all properties of a specific node including transform, visibility, physics, and custom properties. Returns typed property values.

project_path.description: Absolute path to the Godot project

node_path.description: Path to the node within the scene tree

toolPurpose: Obtain the complete hierarchical scene tree structure. Returns node names, types, and parent-child relationships for the entire scene.

project_path.description: Absolute path to the Godot project

scene_path.description: Path to the scene file to inspect

toolPurpose: Bind a GDScript file to a specific node in a scene. Creates the linkage between logic (script) and visual element (node).

project_path.description: Absolute path to the Godot project

node_path.description: Path to the target node in the scene tree

script_path.description: Path to the GDScript file to attach (e.g., 'res://scripts/player.gd')

toolPurpose: Read the contents of a GDScript file. Returns the full source code for analysis, debugging, or modification planning.

project_path.description: Absolute path to the Godot project

toolPurpose: Update the contents of an existing GDScript file. Replaces the script source code with new content. Use after reading the current script to make targeted changes.

project_path.description: Absolute path to the Godot project

script_path.description: Path to the GDScript file to modify

content.description: Complete new GDScript source code to write to the file

toolPurpose: Generate a new GDScript file with specified content. Creates the file at the given path within the project.

project_path.description: Absolute path to the Godot project

content.description: GDScript source code for the new file

base_class.description: Base class the script extends (e.g., 'Node2D', 'CharacterBody2D'). Used if content is not provided to generate a template.

toolPurpose: Persist current scene modifications to disk. Supports save-as to a new path for creating scene variants.

project_path.description: Absolute path to the Godot project

scene_path.description: Path to the scene to save

output_path.description: Optional alternative path for 'save-as' (creates a copy at the new location)

toolPurpose: Apply a texture to a Sprite2D, Sprite3D, or TextureRect node. Loads an image file and assigns it as the node's texture property.

project_path.description: Absolute path to the Godot project

scene_path.description: Path to the scene containing the sprite node

node_path.description: Path to the sprite node in the scene tree

toolPurpose: Convert a 3D scene into a MeshLibrary resource for use with GridMap. Essential for creating tilemap-like 3D level editors.

project_path.description: Absolute path to the Godot project

scene_path.description: Path to the 3D scene containing meshes to export

output_path.description: Output path for the .meshlib resource file (e.g., 'res://resources/tiles.meshlib')

toolPurpose: Capture a PNG screenshot of the running game viewport. Provides visual feedback for the Mind to verify game state, UI layout, and visual elements during playtesting.

project_path.description: Path to the running project

toolPurpose: Execute batched input sequences on a running game. Supports key presses, mouse clicks, mouse movement, UI element clicks by name/path, Godot action presses, and timed waits. Essential for automated playtesting.

project_path.description: Path to the running project

actions.description: Ordered array of input actions to execute

element.description: UI element name or path for ui_click

action.description: Godot input action name for action_press (e.g., 'ui_accept', 'move_left')

duration.description: Duration in seconds for wait actions or key hold time

toolPurpose: Identify all visible Control nodes in the running game. Returns element names, types, positions, text content, and disabled state. Useful for UI testing and navigation.

project_path.description: Path to the running project

toolPurpose: Compile and execute arbitrary GDScript at runtime with full SceneTree access. Enables live code injection for testing, debugging, and dynamic game modification during gameplay.

project_path.description: Path to the running project

code.description: GDScript code to execute at runtime. Has access to the full SceneTree and all loaded nodes.

toolPurpose: Access Godot project configuration parameters including display settings, input map, physics configuration, rendering options, and custom project settings.

project_path.description: Absolute path to the Godot project

toolPurpose: Enumerate all GDScript files within the project. Returns paths, base classes, and basic metadata for each script.

project_path.description: Absolute path to the Godot project

toolPurpose: List all scene files (.tscn, .scn) within the project. Returns scene paths and root node types for project navigation.

project_path.description: Absolute path to the Godot project

toolPurpose: List all available game templates in the template library. Returns template IDs, names, genres, dimensions, descriptions, pre-built mechanics, and example prompts. Used by the Mind to find the best starting point for a user's game concept before building from scratch.

genre_filter.description: Optional genre to filter templates by (e.g., 'platformer', 'rpg', 'puzzle', 'endless_runner'). If omitted, returns all templates.

toolPurpose: Clone a template project into a new working directory, creating a fully functional Godot project pre-loaded with scenes, scripts, assets, and mechanics defined in the template. The Mind then customizes this project based on the user's specific requirements rather than building from scratch.

template_id.description: Template identifier to clone (e.g., 'TPL-GODOT-001' for platformer, 'TPL-GODOT-002' for RPG, 'TPL-GODOT-003' for puzzle, 'TPL-GODOT-004' for endless runner)

project_name.description: Name for the new project (used as directory name and project.godot display name)

output_directory.description: Filesystem path where the new project directory will be created
